sections.paragraph:
fut. (יַחְשֹׁב but יַחֲשָׁב־ Ps. 40:18 יַחֲשֹׁבוּן Psa 35:20)-

(1) to think, to meditate. (Arab. حَسَبَ, Syr. ܚܫܰܒ, Eth. ሐሰበ፡ and ሐዘበ፡ id. The primary idea seems to be that of computing, reckoning, see Piel No. 1; hence, to reckon with; unless perhaps it be that of mixing, like Arab. خشب and اشب, whence חשֵׁב a weaver in coloured figures, properly, one mixing threads and colours). Isa 10:7 Gen 50:20. Followed by an acc. i.q. to think out, to invent, to compose, as songs [music], Amo 6:5 artificial work (compare חִשָּׁבֹון), Exo 31:4 whence חשֵׁב an artificer, 2Ch 26:15 especially polymitarius, a weaver of damask adorned with figures (different from רֹקֵם) Exo 26:1, 31 Exo 26:31 28:6 35:35 36:8 39:8 . More frequently used in a bad sense; to devise evil, to plot, as חָשַׁב אָוֶן, ח׳ מְזִמָּה Psa 10:2 21:12 35:20 36:5 52:4 חָשַׁב רָעָה עַל Gen 50:20 Mic 2:3 Nah 1:11 חָשַׁב מַחֲשָׁבֹות עַל (against some one), Jer 11:19 18:11 , 18 Jer 18:18followed by אֶל Jer 49:20 50:45 followed by a gerund, to think, to purpose to do something, Psa 140:5 1Sa 18:25 Jer 18:8 26:3 36:3 Job 6:26 Est 9:24 (where there is added עַל of the person).

(2) to think, to take to be so and so, followed by acc. and dat. (λογίζεσθαί τινα εἰς τι). Gen 38:15, וַיַּחְשְׁבֶהָ לְזֹונָה “and he thought her (or, took her for) a harlot.” 1Sa 1:13 Job 13:24 19:15 33:10 35:2 41:19 , 24 Job 41:24followed by an acc. and כְּ Job 19:11. Absol. to make much account of, to esteem, to prize (achten for hochachten). Isa 13:17, אֲשֶׁר כֶּסֶף לֹא יַחְשֹׁבוּ “who do not regard silver.” Isa 33:8 53:3 Mal 3:16.

(3) to impute something to some one; followed by לְ of pers. and acc. of the thing; e.g. sin, Psa 32:2 2Sa 19:20 a good deed, [which was not any work at all, but simply his believing God], Gen 15:6.

Niphal-

(1) pass. of Piel No. 1, to be computed, reckoned, 2Ki 22:7 to be accounted, followed by לְ to, Jos 13:3 עַל 2Sa 4:2.

(2) pass. of Kal No. 2, to be taken for,-followed by an acc. Pro 17:28, “even a fool while he is silent חָכָם יֵחָשֵׁב is counted wise.” Gen 31:15 Isa 40:15. Followed by כְּ to be reckoned equal to some one, Job 18:3 41:21 Hos 8:12 (hence to be like, Isa 5:28); followed by לְ id. 1Ki 10:21 Lam 4:2. בְּ Isa 2:22, בַּמֶּה נֶחְשָׁב הוּא “to what shall he be made equal,” i.e. at how much is he to be estimated? followed by עִם Psa 88:5.

(3) pass. of Kal No. 3, to be imputed to some one, followed by לְ Lev 7:18 17:4 Num 18:27 Psa 106:31.

Piel-

(1) to compute, to reckon; (as to the primary signification of roots being very often preserved in Piel, see Lehrg. p. 242); with acc. (etwas berechnen, ausrechnen) Lev 25:27, 50 Lev 25:50, 52 Lev 25:52 27:18 , 23 Lev 27:23, אֵת (אִתּ) to reckon with any, 2Ki 12:16.

(2) to consider, to think upon (bedenken), Psa 77:6 119:59 .

(3) to think, to meditate, i.q. Kal No. 1, absol. Psa 73:16 followed by an acc. to think out, Pro 16:9 in a bad sense, to devise, to plot, followed by עַל of pers. חִשַּׁב מַחֲשָׁבֹות עַל Dan 11:24 אֶל of pers. Nah 1:9 Hos 7:15. Metaph. of inanimate things, to be as though it were- Jon 1:4, “the ship was as though it would be broken.”

Hithpael reflex. i.q. Niphal No. 1, to reckon one’s self with. Num 24:9.

The derivatives follow, exc. חַשּׁוּב, מַחֲשָׁבָה.
